openarchiver.name: openarchiver
openarchiver.short_description: Open Archiver is the leading open-source email archiving solution. A secure, self-hosted platform for email backup, compliance, and eDiscovery with full-text search ability. Get this free email archiver today.

features.description: Direct connections to major email platforms.
features.title: Microsoft 365 and Google Workspace connectors

features.description: Import existing archives without a live mailbox connection.
features.title: PST and EML import

pros: Supports both live connections (Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, IMAP) and historical PST/EML imports

faqs.answer: Yes — along with generic IMAP servers and PST/EML file imports.
faqs.question: Does Open Archiver connect to Microsoft 365 and Google Workspace?

zendesk.name: Zendesk
zendesk.short_description: An AI-driven customer service platform unifying ticketing, live chat, messaging, and voice support across channels, with AI agents on higher tiers.

features.description: Ticketing, live chat, messaging, and voice unified in one system.
features.title: Omnichannel support

features.description: Automated first-response handling included from Suite Team and above.
features.title: Built-in AI agents

features.description: Copilot, Workforce Engagement, and Contact Center available as add-ons.
features.title: Separately-priced add-on bundles

pros: AI agents are built into Suite plans rather than sold as a separate expensive add-on
cons: Entry-level Support Team plan lacks the omnichannel and AI capabilities of the Suite tiers, requiring an upgrade for full functionality

faqs.answer: Yes — AI agents for automated first-response are included from the Suite Team plan and above.
faqs.question: Does Zendesk include AI agents?

faqs.answer: Yes — a 14-day free trial is available.
faqs.question: Does Zendesk offer a free trial?

deskpro.name: Deskpro
deskpro.short_description: Multi-channel help desk with a built-in AI chatbot, agent assist co-pilot, and flexible cloud, private-cloud, or self-hosted deployment.

features.description: Combines email, chat, voice, SMS, WhatsApp, and social media in one place.
features.title: Unified Inbox

features.description: Resolves common issues before they reach human agents.
features.title: Deskpro AI Chatbot

features.description: Summarizes tickets, drafts replies, and creates knowledge base articles.
features.title: AI Agent Assist (Co-Pilot)

features.description: Routes tickets automatically based on intent, sentiment, and language.
features.title: AI Workflow Automations

features.description: Customer-facing knowledge base.
features.title: Self-Service Help Center

features.description: Connects ticket workflows to internal collaboration tools.
features.title: Slack & Microsoft Teams Integration

features.description: Apps for managing tickets on the go.
features.title: Mobile Apps

features.description: Cloud, private cloud, self-hosted, on-premise, or sovereign data center options.
features.title: Flexible Deployment

pros: Flexible deployment options including self-hosted and on-premise, uncommon among help desk SaaS tools
SOC II, ISO 27001, and HIPAA certified
Deskpro AI (chatbot, agent assist, automations) is built into the platform rather than a separate product
Enterprise plan supports 22 AWS regions for data residency needs
cons: Team plan requires a minimum of 5 agents and Enterprise requires 25, raising the cost floor for very small teams
Deskpro AI and white-labeling are gated behind the Professional plan and above
Exact free trial length isn't stated on the site

faqs.answer: Yes, a free trial is available, though the exact duration isn't specified on the site.
faqs.question: Does Deskpro offer a free trial?

faqs.answer: Cloud pricing starts at $39/agent/month for the Team plan (minimum 5 agents), up to $99/agent/month for Enterprise (minimum 25 agents).
faqs.question: How much does Deskpro cost?

faqs.answer: Yes, deployment options include cloud, private cloud, self-hosted, on-premise, and sovereign data centers.
faqs.question: Can Deskpro be self-hosted?

faqs.answer: Yes, Deskpro AI includes a chatbot, an agent assist co-pilot, and workflow automations based on intent, sentiment, and language.
faqs.question: Does Deskpro include AI?

faqs.answer: Yes, it is SOC II, ISO 27001, and HIPAA certified.
faqs.question: Is Deskpro compliant with security standards?

defectdojo.name: DefectDojo
defectdojo.short_description: Open-source vulnerability management and ASPM platform for centralizing security findings from 200+ scanning tools.

features.description: Imports results from over 200 security scanning tools and deduplicates overlapping findings.
features.title: Finding import and deduplication

features.description: Supports RBAC alongside authentication via username/password, LDAP, SAML, and OAuth.
features.title: Role-based access control

features.description: Provides a REST API with built-in Swagger documentation for automation and integration.
features.title: REST API and Swagger UI

features.description: Pro tier adds a rules engine, tunable deduplication, and background imports for automated workflows.
features.title: Automation rules engine (Pro)

features.description: Pro tier includes additional integrations such as Snyk, SonarQube, and AWS, plus a universal CSV/JSON parser.
features.title: Premium parsers and integrations (Pro)

features.description: Pro tier can be run as a cloud-hosted SaaS instance in addition to self-hosting.
features.title: Cloud-hosted option (Pro)

pros: Free, self-hosted open-source edition under BSD-3-Clause
Over 200 integrations with security scanning tools
38 million+ downloads and 4,000+ GitHub stars for the open-source project
Storage-based pricing on Pro rather than per-app or per-user fees
Active community support via Slack
cons: Advanced automation, premium parsers, and MFA require the paid Pro tier
Pro pricing beyond the base tier requires a custom quote for larger storage volumes
Self-hosting the open-source edition requires infrastructure and maintenance effort

faqs.answer: Yes, DefectDojo has a free, open-source, self-hosted edition licensed under BSD-3-Clause. A paid Pro tier with additional features starts around $300/month.
faqs.question: Is DefectDojo free?

faqs.answer: The open-source edition is released under the BSD-3-Clause license.
faqs.question: What license is DefectDojo released under?

faqs.answer: DefectDojo offers over 200 integrations with security scanning tools.
faqs.question: How many tools does DefectDojo integrate with?

faqs.answer: Pro adds an automation rules engine, tunable deduplication, background imports, premium parsers, a cloud-hosted option, MFA, and premium support.
faqs.question: What does DefectDojo Pro add over the free edition?

dependency-track.name: Dependency-Track
dependency-track.short_description: Open-source, OWASP-maintained platform that analyzes SBOMs to inventory components and identify supply-chain vulnerabilities.

features.description: Uses CycloneDX SBOMs to track libraries, containers, operating systems, firmware, and services across projects.
features.title: SBOM-based inventory

features.description: Matches inventoried components against multiple vulnerability sources, including NVD, GitHub Advisories, Snyk, and OSV.
features.title: Continuous vulnerability analysis

features.description: An expression-based policy engine enforces security, operational, and license-compliance rules.
features.title: Policy engine

features.description: Version 5.0 adds horizontal scaling and active/active high availability for large deployments.
features.title: Horizontal scalability

features.description: Deploys via Docker or Docker Compose with a PostgreSQL database (v5.0+).
features.title: Docker-based deployment

pros: Free and open source under the Apache 2.0 license
Maintained by OWASP as a Flagship Project
Used by 20,000+ organizations according to the project site
Scales to process tens of thousands of SBOMs per hour
Cross-references multiple vulnerability intelligence sources
cons: No official paid or managed hosting tier from the project itself
Requires self-hosting infrastructure (Docker, PostgreSQL) and ongoing maintenance
Effectiveness depends on consistently generating accurate SBOMs upstream

faqs.answer: Yes. Dependency-Track is free and open source, licensed under Apache 2.0.
faqs.question: Is Dependency-Track free?

faqs.answer: Dependency-Track is maintained as an OWASP Flagship Project.
faqs.question: Who maintains Dependency-Track?

faqs.answer: Dependency-Track ingests CycloneDX SBOMs to build its component inventory.
faqs.question: What format of SBOM does Dependency-Track use?

faqs.answer: It is typically deployed via Docker or Docker Compose, with PostgreSQL as the database for version 5.0 and later.
faqs.question: How is Dependency-Track deployed?

glpi.name: GLPI
glpi.short_description: Open-source IT service management and asset/CMDB platform, self-hosted for free or via paid GLPI Network Cloud plans.

features.description: Manages support tickets alongside IT assets and infrastructure.
features.title: Helpdesk and ticket management

features.description: Tracks hardware, software, and other assets across the organization.
features.title: Asset and inventory management

features.description: Configuration Management Database for tracking infrastructure relationships.
features.title: CMDB

features.description: Scans and inventories cloud resources without installing agents.
features.title: Agentless cloud inventory

features.description: Automates recurring IT service and asset workflows.
features.title: Workflow automation

features.description: iOS and Android mobile applications for on-the-go access.
features.title: Mobile apps

pros: Free, open-source core (GPL-3.0) actively maintained with recent releases (v11.0.8, 2026)
Large established community with 6,100+ GitHub stars
Managed cloud hosting available (GLPI Network Cloud) if self-hosting isn't wanted
Mobile apps for iOS and Android
cons: Cloud plans are priced per IT agent, which can scale up in cost for larger teams
Advanced plugin tiers (Standard, Advanced) require paid GLPI Network subscriptions starting at €100/month
Self-hosted deployment requires server setup and maintenance

faqs.answer: Yes, GLPI's core platform is free and open source (GPL-3.0) to self-host.

faqs.answer: GLPI Network Public Cloud starts at €19/month per IT agent; Private Cloud starts at €21/month per IT agent (minimum 25 agents).
faqs.question: How much does GLPI Cloud cost?

faqs.answer: Yes, GLPI's cloud offering includes a 45-day free trial.
faqs.question: Does GLPI have a free trial?

faqs.answer: Yes, GLPI offers mobile applications for iOS and Android.
faqs.question: Does GLPI have mobile apps?

authelia.name: Authelia
authelia.short_description: Authelia is an open-source authentication and authorization server providing SSO and multi-factor authentication for self-hosted apps via a reverse proxy.

features.description: Centralized authentication portal supporting single sign-on across web applications.
features.title: Multi-factor authentication and SSO

features.description: Supports Passkeys, one-time passwords, mobile push notifications, and WebAuthn.
features.title: Modern authentication methods

features.description: Brute-force prevention through login rate regulation.
features.title: Login regulation

features.description: Fine-grained access control policies for users and groups.
features.title: Granular authorization policies

features.description: Operates as an OpenID Certified provider for standards-based SSO.
features.title: OpenID Connect 1.0 provider

features.description: Container image under 20MB with typical memory usage under 30MB.
features.title: Lightweight footprint

features.description: Deployable for high availability on Kubernetes.
features.title: Kubernetes support

pros: Free and open source under the Apache 2.0 license
Very lightweight (container image under 20MB)
Fast performance, with ~100ms portal load and millisecond-level authorization
OpenID Certified provider
Supports modern auth methods like Passkeys and WebAuthn
cons: Requires a reverse proxy to function; not a standalone application
Self-hosted setup and configuration required
Relies on community contributions/donations to fund continued security audits

faqs.answer: Yes, it is open source under the Apache 2.0 license.
faqs.question: Is Authelia free?

faqs.answer: Passkeys, one-time passwords, mobile push notifications, and WebAuthn.
faqs.question: What authentication methods does Authelia support?

faqs.answer: No, it is designed as a companion to a reverse proxy.

faqs.answer: Yes, it operates as an OpenID Certified provider.

faqs.answer: The container image is under 20MB and typically uses under 30MB of memory.

thehive.name: TheHive
thehive.short_description: TheHive is a collaborative security incident response and case management platform for SOCs, CSIRTs, and CERTs, developed by StrangeBee.

features.description: Organizes SOC work around Cases, Tasks, and Observables, letting teams triage alerts and track incident-response activity.
features.title: Case and alert management

features.description: Integrates with Cortex, StrangeBee's analysis and active-response engine, to automatically enrich and analyze indicators such as IPs, hashes, and URLs.
features.title: Observable analysis via Cortex

features.description: Connects to MISP threat intelligence servers to import and correlate indicators of compromise directly within cases.
features.title: MISP integration

features.description: StrangeBee's site lists 300+ integrations for pulling alerts from other security tools into TheHive.
features.title: 300+ integrations

features.description: Available as a self-hosted on-premises install, prebuilt cloud images for AWS/Azure, or a fully managed TheHive Cloud Platform SaaS.
features.title: Multiple deployment options

pros: Free Community edition is available for self-hosting, supporting up to 2 users and 1 organization
Deep native integration with Cortex and MISP for threat intelligence and observable analysis
Used by a large community: StrangeBee cites 3,500+ users across 50+ countries
Flexible deployment: self-hosted, cloud images, or managed SaaS
cons: Since StrangeBee took over development in 2018, TheHive 4 and 5 no longer accept external code contributions, and TheHive's earlier fully open-source versions (3 and 4) were discontinued and their repositories archived in 2023
The free Community edition is capped at 2 users and 1 organization; larger teams need a paid Gold or Platinum license
Gold and Platinum plan pricing is not publicly listed and requires contacting StrangeBee
Advanced support (business-hours or priority response) is only available on paid tiers

faqs.answer: TheHive offers a free Community edition limited to 2 users and 1 organization. Paid Gold and Platinum editions remove those limits and add support and enterprise features.
faqs.question: Is TheHive free?

faqs.answer: Cortex is StrangeBee's companion observable-analysis and active-response engine that TheHive uses to automatically enrich indicators of compromise.
faqs.question: What is Cortex?

faqs.answer: TheHive has been developed by StrangeBee since 2018. The current versions (4 and 5) are developed exclusively by StrangeBee's team rather than the open community.
faqs.question: Who develops TheHive?

faqs.answer: Yes, TheHive natively integrates with MISP threat intelligence servers to import and correlate indicators.
faqs.question: Can TheHive integrate with MISP?

features: Business-hours EU support via email, chat, and video, 1-business-day response

misp.name: MISP
misp.short_description: MISP is a free, open-source threat intelligence platform for collecting, correlating, and sharing indicators of compromise among security teams.

features.description: Stores threat indicators in a structured format that enables correlation, automated export for IDS/SIEM systems, and analysis.
features.title: Structured indicator storage

features.description: Exports threat data in open standard formats including STIX and OpenIOC for interoperability with other security tools.
features.title: STIX and OpenIOC export

features.description: Includes classification systems such as MITRE ATT&CK and Exploit-Kit taxonomies to tag and categorize threat data.
features.title: Taxonomies and Galaxy classification

features.description: Supports multi-instance synchronization so organizations can share threat intelligence across trusted communities.
features.title: Community sync and sharing

features.description: Provides a REST API and the PyMISP Python library for programmatic access and automation.
features.title: PyMISP API

pros: Completely free to use, with the software, data format, and API released under open standards and OSI-approved open source licenses
Governed by an interlocked contributor license so no single organization can change MISP's open-source model
Large, active community of users creating and maintaining shared threat-intelligence communities
Broad interoperability via STIX, OpenIOC, and TAXII export formats
cons: Self-hosting and configuring MISP requires security and infrastructure expertise
No official managed SaaS hosting from the MISP project itself; commercial hosting/support comes from third parties
Effective threat-intel sharing depends on joining or building trusted communities, which takes ongoing effort

faqs.answer: Yes. MISP is free and open source software, with its data format and API also released as open standards.
faqs.question: Is MISP free?

faqs.answer: MISP supports export to STIX, OpenIOC, and other formats, plus TAXII push for automated distribution to IDS or SIEM systems.
faqs.question: What formats does MISP export to?

faqs.answer: MISP is used by incident analysts, security and ICT professionals, and malware reversers who need structured threat information sharing.
faqs.question: Who uses MISP?

faqs.answer: Yes. MISP supports multi-instance synchronization, allowing organizations to build and operate their own sharing communities.
faqs.question: Can I run my own MISP community?

conjur.name: Conjur
conjur.short_description: Conjur Open Source is CyberArk's secrets management and machine-identity platform for securing credentials in cloud-native environments.

features.description: Provides a REST-based secret store with a role-based access control engine, defined using its Machine Authorization Markup Language (MAML).
features.title: Centralized secret store

features.description: Includes built-in authenticators for Kubernetes, OpenShift, AWS IAM, and OIDC to let workloads fetch secrets without static credentials.
features.title: Machine identity authentication

features.description: Lets applications connect to backend services without ever fetching or managing passwords and keys directly.
features.title: Secretless Broker

features.description: Integrates with Ansible, Jenkins, Puppet, and Terraform, as well as CI/CD and container orchestration platforms.
features.title: DevOps toolchain integrations

features.description: Official client libraries are available for .NET, Go, Java, Python, and Ruby.
features.title: Multi-language client SDKs

pros: Conjur server is licensed under GNU LGPL v3.0, with client libraries under Apache License 2.0
Actively maintained on GitHub, with releases as recent as mid-2026 and over 900 GitHub stars
Built-in authenticators cover major cloud and container platforms (Kubernetes, OpenShift, AWS IAM, OIDC)
Backed by CyberArk, an established identity security vendor, with commercial licenses and support available
cons: CyberArk was acquired by Palo Alto Networks, and the conjur.org marketing domain now redirects to Palo Alto Networks' secrets management product page, which can make it harder to find dedicated Conjur Open Source resources
The full-featured enterprise edition and commercial support require a paid CyberArk/Palo Alto Networks license
Configuration uses a proprietary policy language (MAML) rather than a more widely known format

faqs.answer: Yes. The Conjur server is licensed under GNU LGPL v3.0 and client libraries under Apache License 2.0. Commercial licenses are also available from CyberArk.
faqs.question: Is Conjur open source?

faqs.answer: Conjur is a secrets management and machine-identity platform that stores secrets, authenticates workloads, and enforces role-based access to credentials.
faqs.question: What does Conjur do?

faqs.answer: Conjur includes built-in authenticators for Kubernetes, OpenShift, AWS IAM, and OIDC, among others.
faqs.question: Which platforms can authenticate to Conjur?

faqs.answer: Conjur is maintained by CyberArk, which was acquired by Palo Alto Networks; the open-source project remains actively developed on GitHub.
faqs.question: Who maintains Conjur today?

features: Kubernetes, OpenShift, AWS IAM, OIDC authenticators

osquery.name: osquery
osquery.short_description: osquery is a free, open-source tool that exposes an operating system as a relational database for SQL-based security monitoring.

features.description: Exposes operating system information such as running processes, network connections, and file hashes as queryable SQL tables.
features.title: SQL-based OS instrumentation

features.description: Ships with an extensive schema of pre-built tables representing operating system concepts across supported platforms.
features.title: 100+ built-in tables

features.description: Supports interactive querying through the osqueryi shell and continuous scheduled monitoring via the osqueryd daemon.
features.title: Ad hoc and scheduled queries

features.description: Provides Thrift-based APIs for building custom applications and extensions on top of the query engine.
features.title: Thrift API extensions

features.description: Runs on Linux, macOS, and Windows.
features.title: Cross-platform support

pros: Free and open source, dual-licensed under Apache License 2.0 and GPL 2.0
Governed under an open foundation model (the osquery project moved to the Linux Foundation), providing vendor-neutral governance
Widely adopted, with 23.4k+ GitHub stars and 2.6k+ forks at the time of research
Large, extensible schema (100+ tables) covering processes, network, kernel, and file system data
Regular release cadence, with minor releases roughly every two months
cons: Requires SQL and systems knowledge to write effective queries and schedules
No vendor-run managed service from the osquery project itself; fleet management typically requires a separate tool such as Fleet
Deploying and tuning scheduled queries at scale requires ongoing operational effort

faqs.answer: Yes. osquery is free and open source, dual-licensed under Apache License 2.0 and GPL 2.0.
faqs.question: Is osquery free?

faqs.answer: osquery supports Linux, macOS, and Windows.
faqs.question: What platforms does osquery support?

faqs.answer: osquery can be queried ad hoc via the osqueryi interactive shell, or scheduled continuously via the osqueryd daemon.
faqs.question: How do I query with osquery?

faqs.answer: osquery is a community-driven open-source project with GitHub-based issue tracking and a regular release cadence.
faqs.question: Who governs the osquery project?

suricata.name: Suricata
suricata.short_description: Open-source network threat detection engine that runs as an IDS, IPS, and network security monitor.

features.description: Runs as an IDS that alerts on suspicious traffic or as an inline IPS that can drop traffic in real time, from a single binary.
features.title: Intrusion detection and prevention

features.description: Logs HTTP requests, TLS handshakes, DNS queries, file transfers, and other protocol activity for later analysis.
features.title: Network security monitoring

features.description: Built as a multi-threaded engine designed to scale across modern multi-core hardware for higher-throughput networks.
features.title: Multi-threaded engine

features.description: Uses Snort-style rule syntax, allowing rulesets such as the Emerging Threats Open ruleset to work without modification.
features.title: Snort-compatible rule syntax

features.description: Used directly by organizations and embedded by major security vendors into their own products.
features.title: Community and vendor embedding

pros: Free and open source under GPLv2 with no licensing fee for the engine
Multi-threaded architecture suited to high-throughput networks
Compatible with existing Snort-style rulesets, including Emerging Threats Open
Maintained by the nonprofit Open Information Security Foundation (OISF)
Active community with an annual SuriCon conference
cons: Requires manual rule management and tuning to be effective
No official managed/hosted commercial tier from OISF itself
Effective deployment requires network security expertise

faqs.answer: Yes. Suricata is free and open-source software, licensed under GPLv2, with no fee for the engine.
faqs.question: Is Suricata free to use?

faqs.answer: Suricata is maintained by the Open Information Security Foundation (OISF), a nonprofit foundation.
faqs.question: Who maintains Suricata?

faqs.answer: Suricata uses Snort-style rule syntax, so rulesets like Emerging Threats Open work with it out of the box.
faqs.question: What rule format does Suricata use?

faqs.answer: Yes. Suricata can run inline as an intrusion prevention system (IPS) that drops malicious traffic, in addition to IDS alerting and NSM logging.
faqs.question: Can Suricata block traffic, not just alert?
